surface_forms: your maternal grandfather was James Sinclair
description: Canadian fur trader
description_wiki: James Sinclair (1811 – March 26, 1856) was a trader and explorer with the Hudson's Bay Company (HBC).

description: Canadian retail business group, former fur trading business
description_wiki: The Hudson's Bay Company (abbreviated HBC and colloquially Hudson's Bay) is a Canadian holding company of department stores and commercial property.

description: city in Walla Walla County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Walla Walla ( WAH-lə WAH-lə) is a city in and the county seat of Walla Walla County, Washington, United States.

description: capital and largest city of the province of Manitoba, Canada
description_wiki: Winnipeg ( ) is the capital and largest city of the Canadian province of Manitoba.

description: American actress (1922-2000)
description_wiki: Mary Sinclair (born Ella Delores Cook; November 15, 1922 – November 5, 2000) was an American television, film and stage actress and “a familiar face to television viewers in the 1950s” as a performer in numerous plays produced and broadcast live during the early days of television.

description: capital city of Scotland, UK
description_wiki: Edinburgh is the capital city of Scotland and one of its 32 council areas.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: Scotland is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: primary special-collections library of the University of California, Berkeley
description_wiki: The Bancroft Library is the main special collections library of the University of California, Berkeley.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

description: American fur trade company
description_wiki: The American Fur Company (AFC) was a prominent American company that sold furs, skins, and buffalo robes.

description: American fur trade company
description_wiki: The American Fur Company (AFC) was a prominent American company that sold furs, skins, and buffalo robes.

description: city in and county seat of Wasco County, Oregon, United States
description_wiki: The Dalles (  DALZ), formally the City of the Dalles and also called Dalles City, is an inland port, the county seat of and the largest city in Wasco County, Oregon, United States.

description: river in North America; flows from British Columbia, through Washington, and along the Washington-Oregon border to the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: The Columbia River (Upper Chinook: Wimahl or Wimal; Sahaptin: Nch’i-Wàna or Nchi wana; Sinixt dialect swah'netk'qhu) is the largest river in the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oregon (  ORR-ih-ghən, -⁠gon) is a state in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States.

description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: president of the United States from 1869 to 1877
description_wiki: Ulysses S. Grant (born Hiram Ulysses Grant; April 27, 1822 – July 23, 1885) was the 18th president of the United States, serving from 1869 to 1877.

description: seat of Clark County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Vancouver (  van-KOO-vər) is a city in Clark County, Washington, United States, located on the north bank of the Columbia River.

description: county in Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Multnomah County   is one of the 36 counties in the U.S. state of Oregon.

description: seat of Multnomah County, and largest city in state of Oregon, United States
description_wiki: Portland ( PORT-lənd) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Oregon.
